authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(110) — Stone Lagoon (Humboldt Co.)
sources.quotedText: (110) Stone Lagoon (Humboldt Co.). All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. Cutthroat Trout minimum size limit: 14 inches. 2 Cutthroat Trout. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40 — Hatchery Trout/Steelhead Footnote (**) Definition
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

sources.title: Humboldt Lagoons State Park — California State Parks
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS gaz_id 235524, "Stone Lagoon", Lake, Humboldt County, CA — coordinate cross-check (HomeTownLocator, GNIS fallback since gaz_id 235524 is absent from the geonames ArcGIS MapServer/7 mirror)
sources.quotedText: Category: California physical, cultural and historic features; Feature Name: Stone Lagoon, Type: Physical, Class: Lake, County: Humboldt, Nearest Prominent Town: Orick, CA, FID: 235524, Coordinates: Latitude 41.2422834 and Longitude -124.0928113.

groups.humanSummary: Stone Lagoon (Humboldt Co.): open all year, artificial lures with barbless hooks only, 14-inch minimum size on cutthroat trout, 2 cutthroat trout daily, and a separate 2 hatchery trout/steelhead daily (4 in possession) limit.
groups.verbatimText: (110) Stone Lagoon (Humboldt Co.). Open Season/Restrictions: All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. Cutthroat Trout minimum size limit: 14 inches. Daily Bag/Possession Limit: 2 Cutthroat Trout. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Cutthroat trout minimum size limit: 14 inches.
regulations.verbatimText: Cutthroat Trout minimum size limit: 14 inches.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 cutthroat trout daily limit, separate from the hatchery trout/steelhead limit.
Combined limit of hatchery trout and/or hatchery steelhead (fish showing a healed adipose fin clip); 2 fish daily, 4 fish in possession, separate from the cutthroat trout limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.condition: Trout or steelhead lacking a healed adipose fin clip (i.e. wild/unmarked fish)
parameters.note: Only hatchery-marked fish (healed adipose fin clip) may be retained under the hatchery trout/steelhead limit; all other trout and steelhead must be released immediately unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery trout/steelhead may be kept under that limit; unmarked wild fish must be released immediately.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
